theme.title: Rotation: Growth → Value & Defensives
theme.summary: Energy (+3.30%) and Consumer Staples (+0.70%) led the market today, while Technology (-2.59%) and Consumer Discretionary (-1.44%) brought up the rear. That split lines up with a rotation out of growth: growth-oriented sectors averaged -1.38% versus +0.56% for defensives and +0.65% for value and cyclicals, suggesting money moved from high-multiple names into steadier, cash-generative businesses. That shows up at the index level too: the blue-chip Dow gained -0.26% while the tech-heavy Nasdaq fell -1.55%, a wide enough gap to matter. Small caps moved roughly in line with the broader market, with the Russell 2000 at -0.83%. The VIX rose 14.10% to 17.16, pointing to a bit more caution creeping in beneath the surface. Gold slipped 1.54% to $4,004.80, crude oil was little changed at $78.93, and bitcoin slipped 2.63% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
